game_name: King's Quest V: Absence Makes the Heart Go Yonder

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Serves as narrative anchor; player learns it has been stolen
Established as primary threat via castle theft; motivation revealed progressively

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early game, immediately after castle disappearance
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial guide and ongoing commentary; provides advice and can be rescued from danger
Grants initial equipment (wand, white snake piece) and magical guidance
Enables gathering information from NPCs; core utility item
Open-ended exploration area; player gathers items and information
Require inventory puzzle-solving and navigation skill
Primary antagonist's lair; final dungeon environment
Manual-based in floppy version (copy protection); essential for final confrontation
Endgame challenge; player uses Mordack's own spell book spells against him
Plot-relevant NPC; assists in final confrontation; sets up sequel

typical_relief_triggers: Companion commentary and support from Cedric
typical_tension_triggers: Spell casting requirements and magical threats
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gradual escalation from safe tutorial through open exploration to dangerous late-game encounters; narrative tension maintained via companion dialogue and plot revelations about family imprisonment; tension peaks during final magical duel

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Serenia landscape supports exploratory play; optional NPCs and items discoverable through traversal

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction with tutorial support from Cedric and Crispin; emphasis on gathering items and learning mechanics; safe exploration-focused
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate challenge; expanded Serenia exploration requires puzzle-solving and inventory management; environmental hazards increase
difficulty_curve.late_game: Significant difficulty spike; spell casting mastery required; magical duel against Mordack demands learned spells and strategy
notable_spikes: Transition from safe zones to dangerous Serenia exploration
Final magical duel with Mordack (transformation into ring of fire)
Spell casting requirement (manual-dependent in floppy version)

notable_level_patterns.description: Early game area with helpful NPCs (Cedric, Crispin) providing guidance, equipment, and narrative context; establishes mechanics and story motivation
Mid-game Serenia landscape supporting non-linear exploration, NPC interaction via animal communication tool, and distributed item collection
Late-game dungeon environment (Mordack's castle on his island); contains imprisoned family members, spell book resources, and climactic boss encounter
Boss encounter requiring learned spell casting against transforming antagonist; narrative-climactic moment with life-or-death stakes
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mordack's throne room / final battle arena
